summary: local-mcp performs host reconnaissance and POSTs to hardcoded Railway-hosted endpoint on install
details: The package ships three install/runtime scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine child_process execution, filesystem access, os.homedir()/os module reads, and HTTP/HTTPS network calls against a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app in addition to https://local-mcp.com and https://download.local-mcp.com. setup.js contains multiple POST call sites (lines 61, 345, 363, 787, 865) alongside child_process and fs usage, and index.js dispatches on process.platform and reads os.homedir() before making outbound https.get calls to the Railway-hosted host. The package's declared purpose (an MCP helper) does not require a third-party Railway app endpoint co-located with local host enumeration and shell execution, and this destination is not the package's own local-mcp.com domain used for downloading its distribution artifacts. The composition of installer-host inspection with an off-brand hardcoded C2 endpoint reachable from install-time code is the exfiltration shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
